phases.name: Core Foundation
phases.description: Binary starts, database works, providers connect, sessions stream. The minimum viable agent platform.

phases.name: Agent Tools & Observability
phases.description: Agents can execute tools server-side. Traces and journey view provide visibility into agent behavior.

phases.name: Frontend
phases.description: Browser UI for creating sessions, chatting with agents, and observing their work.

phases.name: Kanban
phases.description: Kanban boards with task management and lane automation that triggers specialist sessions.

phases.name: Extended Features
phases.description: Agent-to-agent communication, notes, artifacts, codebase management, and production readiness.

phases.name: Native Tool Loop
phases.description: Complete the native Anthropic tool-execution loop. Prerequisite for all wrapped-CLI work because journey and trace shapes depend on tools actually executing and being recorded. Anthropic API in native mode must keep its current behavior and gain a real tool-use loop.

phases.name: Multi-Runtime Foundation
phases.description: Schema, model, and trait surface changes to carry runtime/mode on a session and a per-Runtime-Tool provider config. No user-facing surface change yet. The Provider table widens to a discriminated union (HTTP vs CLI); the CodingAgent trait gains a per-turn context; runtime x mode compatibility is enforced at session creation.

phases.name: Claude Code CLI Wrapped Mode
phases.description: First CLI runtime target. Per-turn subprocess runner, fake-CLI test harness, Claude Code stream-json parser, PermissionMapper, CLI-native resume id persistence with message-history replay fallback, CLI -> journey translation, child-process reaping on crash, workspace-scoped CLI session validation, and an end-to-end CliCodingAgent conformance test. Anthropic API in native mode stays green throughout.

phases.name: Multi-Runtime User Surface
phases.description: Settings, session creation, kanban, and A2A surfaces that let a user pick a Runtime Tool explicitly. The silent first-provider fallback is replaced with explicit Runtime Tool selection or a configured default.

phases.name: Additional CLI Runtimes
phases.description: Codex and OpenCode adapters, built on the shared CliRunner, parser framework, PermissionMapper, fake-CLI harness, and conformance suite from Phase 8. Each adapter must pass the conformance suite to ship. Real CLI smoke tests are manual.

phases.name: Attended Mode (Deferred)
phases.description: Attended mode is a separate Terminal abstraction (not a CodingAgent impl). Deferred until Phase 8 wrapped mode is stable and the lifecycle split (Weave drives the CLI vs. the user drives the CLI) is justified by real usage.

phases.name: Kanban Ă— Claude Code Coordination
phases.description: Turn the kanban from a fire-and-forget trigger into a coordination surface between the board and the Claude Code agent. Splits docs/design-docs/kanban-claude-code-coordination.md into 5 implementation tasks, each â‰¤1000 lines of changes: rich prompt with stage + gate + history context (feat-063), six new kanban tools callable by the agent (feat-064, may need a sub-split), per-column stage enum with stage-aware prompts and tool filtering (feat-065), full transition-gate enforcement with delivery/contract/checklist/validator rules and a gate_mode toggle (feat-066), and lifecycle supervision (watchdog, recovery re-prompt, failure broadcast) for kanban-auto-spawned sessions (feat-067).

features.behavior: Frontend served from Rust binary. crates/weave-server/build.rs runs `bunx vite build` in ../../web at compile time (path resolved via env!(\\\"CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR\\\"), CWD-independent) and watches web/src, web/index.html, web/package.json, web/vite.config.ts, web/tsconfig.json, and web/bun.lock for changes. The build script invokes vite directly rather than `bun run build` to skip the pre-existing `tsc -b` type-check (the bundle is what gets embedded; full type-check runs in `just lint`/`just test`). Dev mode unchanged: Vite dev server with /api proxy to backend, no build.rs involvement. Tests: tower::ServiceExt::oneshot asserts GET / serves index.html, GET /sessions/abc-123 and GET /favicon.ico fall back to index.html with 200, GET /assets/<hashed>.js returns the JS bytes, and GET /api/health still returns the API route's response.

features.behavior: Re-implement session chat streaming for smooth UX. (1) New SSE event `message_persisted` carrying the persisted row id, role, content, stop_reason, created_at is broadcast AFTER MessageStore::create returns and BEFORE the terminal `done` event. (2) On cancel and on streaming error, the partial streamed text is persisted with stop_reason=cancelled|error encoded in the existing messages.metadata column. (3) Frontend useSession is a useReducer with a discriminated Action union; content-equality dedup in LiveAssistantMessage is replaced with id-based handoff (`liveBubbleId !== persistedTurnId`). (4) dead state removed (thinkingChunks). (5) On user scroll-up while streaming, a 'jump to latest' pill appears. (6) Initial loading renders a 3-row skeleton, not a centered spinner. (7) Thinking blocks render inline and collapsible.

features.behavior: Complete the native Anthropic tool-execution loop in `AnthropicAgent::send_message`. The provider advertises Weave tools via MessageRequest.tools; on a `content_block_start` of type `tool_use` the agent collects the streamed `input_json_delta` events into a serde_json::Value, runs the named tool through ToolRegistry subject to the session's profile, appends a `tool_result` content block carrying the tool_use_id and the serialized result, and continues the model call. The `tool_use` `stop_reason` is the loop driver; the loop terminates on `end_turn` / `max_tokens` / `cancelled` / loop-limit-exceeded. Reuses TraceCollector to record tool_call events with name, sanitized input, output, and duration_ms. features.behavior: Per-turn CLI subprocess runner used by every `CliCodingAgent`. New `CliRunner` in `crates/weave-server/src/agent/cli_runner.rs`. Takes a `CliInvocation { binary, args, env, cwd, stdin_prompt }` plus a `TurnContext` (for cancellation, session id tracking, and the active permission snapshot). Spawns via `tokio::process::Command`, captures stdout as a line stream (one JSON event per line for Claude Code's `stream-json` grammar; the line-stream is the parser's input, not the runner's concern), captures stderr to a bounded buffer (256KB, then truncated with a clear warning), waits on the child with `tokio::select!` on the cancel token, and on cancel sends SIGTERM to the child and SIGKILL after 5s. On exit, the child handle is removed from the per-session process table. The runner is intentionally per-turn: there is no long-lived process state and the child is reaped before the next turn starts. Exit code 0 is success; non-zero is mapped to `StreamEvent::Error` and the runner's stream is closed. Reuses `shell_exec`'s output truncation policy (100KB on stdout, last-256KB on stderr).

features.behavior: A line-stream parser that consumes Claude Code's `stream-json` output and converts each line into a `StreamEvent`. Handles: `text_delta` -> `TextDelta`; `tool_use` start (with `name` and empty `input`) -> `ToolUseStart`; `input_json_delta` -> `ToolUseDelta`; `tool_result` (with `tool_use_id` and `content`) -> `ToolResult`; `thinking` -> `Thinking`; `error` -> `Error`; `session_id` -> captured into `TurnContext::cli_resume_id` (so feat-047 can persist it); `done` (with `stop_reason`) -> `Done`. The parser is a state machine that tracks in-flight `content_block`s by their CLI-assigned ids and reconciles them with the Weave `tool_use` id. Malformed lines (invalid JSON, unknown event types, partial blocks) are logged at WARN and skipped — never fatal. The parser does NOT execute tools; that is the journey translator's job in feat-048. The parser is the only Claude-Code-specific piece; the rest of the runtime is shared with Codex and OpenCode via feat-057.

features.behavior: `PermissionMapper` trait + Claude Code implementation. Trait: `fn effective_permissions(kind: RuntimeKind, profile: ToolProfile) -> PermissionSnapshot`. Snapshot is a struct of CLI-specific flags the runner must build into argv / env. Claude Code mapper: takes the Weave `ToolProfile` (`full` | `implementation` | `review` | `planning` | `reporting`) and emits the corresponding Claude Code `--permission-mode` value, an allowlist of Weave tool names, and the CLI flags the user agreed to. Concrete mapping table lives in `crates/weave-server/src/agent/permissions/claude_code.rs`. The snapshot is passed through `TurnContext::effective_permissions` (feat-041) and consumed by the runner when building argv (feat-043). Review profile maps to `plan`; implementation maps to `accept-edits`; reporting maps to `default` with no shell. Mapping is intentionally minimal: it does NOT try to mirror Weave's exact tool allowlist into Claude Code's tool list — Claude Code's tool surface is the CLI's responsibility. A snapshot is also a struct that can serialize to JSON for debugging (logged at DEBUG on each turn).

features.behavior: CLI-native resume metadata persistence with message-history replay fallback. On a successful CLI turn, the CLI's native session id (captured by the parser in feat-045) is stored in `sessions.runtime_metadata_json['cli_resume_id']`. On the next `send_message` for the same session, the runner reads the stored id and passes it to the CLI via the resume flag the CLI expects (e.g., `--resume <id>` for Claude Code). If the CLI rejects the resume id (non-zero exit, or a structured `resume_unknown_session` error in stdout / stderr), the runner clears the stored id and falls back to message-history replay: rebuild the assistant context from the stored messages table and re-invoke the CLI without the resume flag. The `done` SSE event includes a `resume_state: ` `native` | `replayed` | `none` `` so the UI can surface which mode was used. If the user changes `runtime_kind` on a new session, the prior `cli_resume_id` is NOT inherited. The session header pill (feat-054) shows the resume state.

features.behavior: `JourneyTranslator` that maps parsed CLI stream events into Weave trace events and `StreamEvent`s WITHOUT re-executing CLI tools. Text deltas pass through as `TextDelta`. Tool calls from the CLI (text + input from `ToolUseStart` + `ToolUseDelta`) become a `tool_call` trace event with the CLI's reported input and a placeholder duration captured from the time between `ToolUseStart` and the matching `ToolResult`; the trace is recorded in the `traces` table; `StreamEvent` is `ToolUseStart` / `ToolUseDelta` for UI display. The CLI is the source of truth for the tool result — the translator does NOT re-run the tool. File changes announced by the CLI (via a CLI-specific event type emitted by Claude Code) are recorded in the `file_changes` table. Thinking blocks become `decision` trace events. Errors become `error` trace events. The journey sidebar endpoint (feat-022) is unchanged. If a tool_use does not get a matching tool_result within the turn (CLI dropped it), the translator records a `tool_call` event with `status='orphaned'` so the journey still shows the attempt.

features.behavior: Child-process reaping on crash + per-session process tracking. Extends the existing `ActiveSessions` (added in feat-034) with a parallel `ActiveChildProcesses` table: `session_id -> tokio::process::Child handle + Pid`. New `service::startup::reap_cli_processes` (separate from `reap_orphans` for sessions) scans the filesystem for surviving CLI binaries whose parent pid is the Weave pid and whose session id matches a session that is no longer active (DB row terminal or missing) — terminates them with SIGTERM, waits 5s, then SIGKILL. Cancel routes SIGTERM to the tracked process and removes the entry on exit. Database-only orphan reaping (feat-034) is no longer sufficient once wrapped CLIs exist. Reaping is idempotent and re-runnable. Logs a count of reaped processes. Does not scan processes unrelated to Weave (filters on `parent_pid == weve_pid` and `argv[0]` matching a known registered CLI binary).

New `service::startup::reap_cli_processes` (~200 LoC, 5 tests) in `service/startup.rs` — synchronous, takes `&Db`, returns `Result<ReapSummary, AppError>`; builds the argv[0] basename allowlist from `ProviderStore::list` filtered to `kind='cli'`; walks `/proc/<pid>/status` for `PPid == getpid()` and `/proc/<pid>/cmdline` (raw bytes, NUL-safe) for argv[0] basename; SIGTERM → 5s sleep → SIGKILL via `libc::kill` (direct child; killpg would fail with ESRCH for children spawned without `process_group(0)`); idempotent. `agent::cli_runner` refactored: `CliRunner` now holds an `Arc<ActiveChildProcesses>` field set via `with_registry(...)` (production shares AppState's registry) or `new()` (tests use a fresh local registry); `CliRunner::run` registers on spawn and unregisters on every exit path; `active_count`/`active_pids` inspectors preserved as thin delegators so the 14 existing cli_runner tests and 6 fake_cli tests continue to compile unchanged. `main.rs` wires the new field into `AppState` and calls `reap_cli_processes(&db)` immediately after `reap_orphans` in the startup sequence (before bind, same slot). `api/sessions::cancel_session` calls `state.active_child_processes.terminate(session_id)` as a defense-in-depth path BEFORE the existing `SessionService::cancel_session` (the primary signal is still the `CancellationToken`; terminate is a SIGTERM fallback). All 9 AppState literal sites updated (main.rs + kanban_test_helpers + 7 api test fixtures). Limitation documented in `reap_cli_processes` doc: on Linux, when a parent dies, surviving children are reparented to PID 1, so the literal `PPid == getpid()` filter only catches children of the *current* Weave process — a true 'prior-crash orphan' is not caught. features.behavior: Workspace-scoped CLI session validation. Wrapped sessions (any `mode=wrapped` + any CLI runtime_kind) MUST have `cwd` inside a registered `Codebase` row for the workspace (the existing `codebases.path` unique on `(workspace_id, path)` index is the lookup key — use canonical path comparison via `dunce::canonicalize` to handle Windows path quirks even though Weave is Linux-first). Reject at session creation with `AppError::Validation` and `code: "cwd_outside_codebase"` listing the cwd and the registered codebases for the workspace. Native (HTTP) sessions keep their existing behavior (cwd is optional and unconstrained). Kanban auto-spawn calls the same validator before creating a wrapped session. The provider's default working directory (if the provider row has one) is used as a fallback cwd inside the first matching codebase. A2A `POST /api/a2a/messages` also enforces the check.

features.behavior: A shared CLI adapter conformance test suite that every `CliCodingAgent` impl must pass before shipping. The suite lives in `crates/weave-server/tests/cli_conformance.rs` and exercises the adapter contract independent of which CLI is being wrapped. Conformance cases: (a) argv construction from a `CliInvocation` matches the recorded expected argv; (b) the stream parser maps a canonical event sequence to the expected `StreamEvent` sequence; (c) the resume id is captured and persisted; (d) the `PermissionMapper` produces a `PermissionSnapshot` whose serialized form matches the expected JSON for each `ToolProfile`; (e) the journey translator records the canonical tool_call and file_change events; (f) the runner handles `permission_denied` and `crash` scenarios from the fake CLI harness (feat-044) by mapping them to `StreamEvent::Error` and terminating the stream; (g) the adapter rejects sessions whose `cwd` is outside a registered codebase (the validation is enforced upstream in feat-050; the conformance test confirms the adapter's `TurnContext` plumbing is correct). Codex (feat-058) and OpenCode (feat-059) must pass the full suite. Real CLIs are not required for the suite — the fake harness is the conformance target.

features.behavior: Attended mode: a separate `Terminal` trait (NOT a `CodingAgent` impl) representing user-driven, long-lived subprocess hosting. The `Terminal` trait: `spawn(session, binary, args, env, cwd) -> TerminalHandle`, `attach(session_id) -> TerminalStream` (a duplex stream the frontend can render in an xterm.js pane), `detach(session_id)`, `kill(session_id) -> Result`. Lifecycle: spawn on session start, attach on session page open, detach on page close, kill on session cancel / shutdown. Watches the filesystem (via the `notify` crate) and the CLI's trace events to feed the journey sidebar — does NOT parse CLI stream events for execution (that's the wrapped-mode translator's job). The session page layout switcher (feat-054) renders the `attended` layout from the same `/session/:id` URL. Phase 11: do not start until Phase 8 wrapped mode is stable. Conformance test uses the fake CLI harness running in a long-lived mode (the harness from feat-044 is extended to support a `serve` subcommand that emits events as they occur and reads stdin for the user's keystrokes).

features.behavior: Wire the existing-but-unused `find_by_cwd_prefix` helper to sessions: a session may be **bound** to a registered codebase (git repo) at create time, and the binding becomes a runtime sandbox for the agent's FS tools. Implementation: (1) New migration `010_session_codebase_id.sql` adds `codebase_id TEXT REFERENCES codebases(id) ON DELETE SET NULL` to `sessions` plus an index. The FK is `SET NULL` so deleting a codebase unbinds the session but does not delete it. (2) `store/sessions.rs` `Session` struct gains `codebase_id: Option<String>`; all SELECT/INSERT queries, `map_row`, `create`, `create_tx` updated to round-trip the new column. (3) `api/sessions.rs` `CreateSessionRequest` gains `codebase_id: Option<String>`; the handler passes it through to the service. (4) `service/sessions.rs` `create_session` resolves `codebase_id` via `CodebaseStore::get_in_workspace` (rejects cross-workspace and unknown ids with `AppError::NotFound`); on success, the codebase's `path` is copied onto the session's `cwd` (the binding wins over any supplied `cwd`). (5) `service/sessions.rs` `run_prompt_task` sets `ToolContext.codebase_root = session.cwd` when the session is bound (and `.` when unbound); the FS tools consult this for containment. The runtime falls back to `session.cwd` as the containment root even when the bound codebase has been deleted (so the sandbox does not silently flip off). (6) `tools/fs/mod.rs` gains a new `validate_read_path` helper (sibling to `validate_write_path`) that enforces containment but skips the control-plane guard — reads of `CLAUDE.md`/`Cargo.toml` etc. are still allowed. `fs_read`, `fs_list`, and `fs_search` (explicit-path branch) all call it. (7) `tools/shell.rs` and `tools/git/mod.rs` `validate_git_cwd` call `validate_read_path` on the explicit-`cwd` form so `shell_exec { cwd: '/etc' }` and `git_log { cwd: '/etc' }` are rejected for bound sessions. (8) `tools/fs/list.rs` and `tools/fs/search.rs` walkers use `entry.file_type()` (not `path.is_dir()`) and skip symlinks entirely — this is the load-bearing piece of the sandbox: `ln -s /etc <repo>/etc_link` cannot be used to escape via `fs_list`/`fs_search`. (9) `store/codebases.rs` drops the `#[allow(dead_code)]` on `find_by_cwd_prefix` since it now has a production caller; the `delete` method's doc comment is updated to reflect the new FK behavior. (10) Frontend: `Session` interface gains `codebase_id: string | null`; `NewSessionModal` adds a `Codebase` dropdown populated by `useCodebases(workspaceId)` with a disabled empty-state + link to `/codebases` when no codebases are registered; on submit, `codebase_id` is sent in the create payload. (11) `app/pages/session.tsx` session header shows a monospace pill with the codebase basename (full path on hover) when `session.cwd` is set. (12) Docs: `docs/user/sessions.md` describes the new `Codebase` field and adds a 'How sessions use a codebase' section that explicitly states the sandbox applies to the cwd arg / fs_read / fs_list / fs_search / explicit-cwd form of shell+git, NOT to the shell command body itself (so a bound session is a permission boundary on cwd, not a jail on the shell). `docs/user/codebases.md` mirrors the same section and updates the 'I registered a codebase but my session does not see it' pitfall. (13) Out of scope: kanban auto-spawn still passes `codebase_id: None` (the `tasks` model has no codebase ref); resume does not auto-inherit parent's binding. Acceptance: `cargo test -p weave-server` and `cd web && bun run test` green, `./init.sh` runs the system smoke (`/api/health` + `curl / | grep 'id="root"'`); user can open `+ New Session`, pick a codebase, submit; the session page header shows the path pill; bound sessions enforce FS containment at the cwd-arg level and refuse to follow symlinks inside the repo.

features.behavior: Rich kanban auto-spawn prompt: replace the 3-line `build_initial_prompt` (crates/weave-server/src/service/kanban.rs:24, returns `"Process task: {title}\n{description}"`) with a structured 10-section prompt builder so the agent knows which column it's in, what gates block the next move, what artifacts are required, and what the previous lane did. Implementation: (1) New file `crates/weave-server/src/service/kanban_prompt.rs` defines `pub struct KanbanPromptContext<'a> { task: &'a Task, column: &'a Column, board: &'a Board, lane_sessions: Vec<LaneSession>, present_artifact_types: HashSet<String>, is_first_lane_run: bool }` and a free `pub fn build_kanban_prompt(ctx: &KanbanPromptContext) -> String`. (2) The 10 sections, in order, are: header line ('You are assigned to Kanban task: {title}'); Context ('You are working in Kanban context. Use tools to manage this card.'); Task Details (Card ID, Board ID, Current Column, Next Column, Priority, Labels, GitHub Issue); Objective (task.objective or task.description); Story Readiness (only when column.stage is 'backlog', derived from task.description's existing acceptance_criteria / verification_commands / test_cases — flags what's present, what's missing); Artifact Gates (current lane's required artifacts + next transition's required artifacts, plus a pointer to list_artifacts / provide_artifact — N/A when no artifacts required); Delivery Gates (only when the next column has delivery rules — committed changes, clean worktree, PR-ready branch); Contract Gates (only when the next column requires the canonical story — description must contain a parseable `yaml` code block); Lane History (previous runs in this lane: provider, role, start/end time, status, summary); Lane Handoff Context (the previous lane's session descriptor and any pending handoffs); Available Tools (column-stage-specific tool list with card-specific IDs); Instructions (column-stage-specific step-by-step guidance, 8-12 steps). (3) The gathering helpers (`fetch_lane_sessions`, `load_present_artifact_types`) live in the same module and pre-load what they need from `TaskStore`, `BoardStore`, `SessionStore`, and `ArtifactStore` so the builder itself stays a pure function over its inputs. (4) `service/kanban.rs::try_automate_lane` builds the `KanbanPromptContext` before calling `SessionService::send_prompt` and passes the rendered string; the old `build_initial_prompt(task: &Task)` function is kept as a thin shim that calls `build_kanban_prompt(&ctx_from_task_only)` so the A2A path (which has no column / board / lane history) keeps working with no behavior change. (5) Specialist `.md` files in `crates/weave-server/resources/specialists/` get a one-line note in their system prompt reminding the model to read the kanban context sections in order (the per-section content itself is dynamic and lives in `kanban_prompt.rs`). Acceptance: `cargo test -p weave-server -- build_kanban_prompt` covers each of the 10 sections in isolation (helper functions return stable strings) and one end-to-end test that calls the old `build_initial_prompt` shim and asserts the new builder is wired in. Existing `test_kanban_autospawn_*` tests stay green (the A2A path uses the shim; the kanban path gets the rich prompt). Line budget: ~700 lines (300 impl + 250 tests + 50 mod wiring + 100 specialist doc updates).
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- build_kanban_prompt && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_kanban_autospawn && ./init.sh

features.behavior: Six kanban tools for agents: `move_card` (already exists at crates/weave-server/src/tools/kanban/move_card.rs:33 and is left untouched), plus 5 new tools that let the agent talk back to the board — `update_card`, `update_task`, `list_artifacts`, `provide_artifact`, `create_note`. **Sub-split warning:** this task is the largest of the phase (~1500 lines estimated: 5 tools × ~250 lines impl + 5 × ~150 tests + ~100 lines of mod wiring). If it exceeds the 1000-line PR budget at implementation time, split into feat-064a (update_card + update_task + create_note, the planning-side tools that mutate the card; ~750 lines) and feat-064b (list_artifacts + provide_artifact, the evidence-side tools that read/write artifacts; ~500 lines). Implementation outline for the full 6-tool set: (1) New files `crates/weave-server/src/tools/kanban/{update_card,update_task,list_artifacts,provide_artifact,create_note}.rs`, each defining a struct that implements `ToolExecutor` (name, description, input_schema, execute). All five delegate to existing store methods — `update_card` → `TaskStore::update` (title/description/priority/labels), `update_task` → `TaskStore::update` (scope/acceptance_criteria/verification_commands/test_cases), `list_artifacts` → `ArtifactStore::list_for_task` (read-only), `provide_artifact` → `ArtifactStore::create` (with mime-type detection), `create_note` → `NoteStore::create` (workspace-scoped). (2) `crates/weave-server/src/tools/kanban/mod.rs` adds `pub mod` + `pub use` for each; the existing `test_kanban_tools` integration test in the `tests` submodule is extended to call all 6 tools in sequence. (3) `crates/weave-server/src/tools/mod.rs` registers the 5 new tools in `ToolRegistry`; profile bindings per design doc §5.2: `full` and `implementation` profiles get all 5; `planning` gets `update_card` + `update_task` + `create_note` but NOT `list_artifacts` / `provide_artifact` (the planning profile is read-only on evidence). (4) Reuse the `require_string` / `optional_string` / `check_optional_status` / `error` / `success` helpers from `tools/fs/mod.rs:1` (already used by `move_card.rs:30`) for input validation — do NOT write new validation glue. (5) The conformance test in `tests/cli_conformance.rs` gains two new cases: a fake CLI emits `tool_use` for `update_card` (assert the task row in DB has the new title) and for `provide_artifact` (assert a new row appears in `artifacts`). Acceptance: all 6 tools appear in `ToolRegistry::all_definitions`; profiles resolve correctly; `cargo test -p weave-server -- test_kanban_tools` covers all 6; conformance test green; `./init.sh` 3-layer gate green.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_kanban_tools && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_tool_registry && cargo test -p weave-server --test cli_conformance && ./init.sh

features.behavior: Column-stage awareness: each column declares a `stage` enum (Backlog | Todo | Dev | Review | Done) that controls prompt variation, available tools, and allowed target-column transitions. Implementation: (1) New migration `crates/weave-server/src/migrations/014_column_stage.sql`: `ALTER TABLE columns ADD COLUMN stage TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'dev'` plus heuristic backfill — `Backlog → backlog`, `To Do → todo`, `In Progress → dev`, `Review → review`, `Done → done`. New columns default to `'dev'` (the most permissive stage) so existing boards are unaffected. (2) `crates/weave-server/src/store/columns.rs::Column` gains `pub stage: ColumnStage`; a new `pub enum ColumnStage { Backlog, Todo, Dev, Review, Done }` with `as_str` / `FromStr` round-trip and a `default()` returning `Dev`. `ColumnStore::create_tx`, `map_row`, and `update` round-trip the new field; the existing `default_column` test helper gets `stage: ColumnStage::default()`. (3) `kanban_prompt.rs::build_kanban_prompt` (from feat-063) reads `column.stage` and varies the `Instructions` section (8-12 stage-specific steps) and the `Available Tools` list (e.g. backlog stage hides `move_card` for non-backlog targets; done stage is read-only). (4) `crates/weave-server/src/tools/kanban/move_card.rs` rejects moves that skip more than one stage — `backlog → dev` is allowed (the common 'refine then start' flow), `backlog → done` is rejected with a clear `AppError::validation` error naming the stage gap. The same-column no-op path is unchanged. (5) `crates/weave-server/src/api/columns.rs` POST and PATCH handlers accept an optional `stage` field (string from the wire; `FromStr` parses; default `dev` when missing). (6) `crates/weave-server/src/store/kanban_test_helpers.rs::default_column` and `seed_workspace_with_board` get `stage: ColumnStage::Dev` to keep existing tests green. Acceptance: `cargo test -p weave-server -- test_columns_migration_adds_stage` mirrors the existing `test_columns_migration_adds_runtime_kind` shape; `test_build_kanban_prompt_stage_backlog` asserts the backlog-stage prompt hides the implementation steps; `test_move_card_stage_filter` asserts `backlog → done` returns the stage-gap error. ./init.sh stays green.
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_columns_migration_adds_stage && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_build_kanban_prompt_stage_backlog && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_move_card_stage_filter && ./init.sh

features.behavior: Full transition-gate enforcement: extend `service::kanban::check_transition_gates` (crates/weave-server/src/service/kanban.rs:312, currently evaluates 3 gates: freeze_description, required_fields, required_artifact_types) to also evaluate 4 new gate types — delivery, contract, checklist, validator — and add a `gate_mode` (blocking | warning) toggle. Implementation: (1) New migration `crates/weave-server/src/migrations/015_column_automation.sql`: `ALTER TABLE columns ADD COLUMN automation_json TEXT` (nullable; NULL = no automation config, the pre-existing behavior). (2) `crates/weave-server/src/store/columns.rs::Column` gains `pub automation: Option<AutomationConfig>`; a new `pub struct AutomationConfig { required_artifacts: Vec<String>, delivery_rules: DeliveryRules, contract_rules: ContractRules, checklist_rules: ChecklistRules, validator_command: Option<String>, gate_mode: GateMode }` with the JSON shape from design doc §5.4. `ColumnStore::create_tx`, `map_row`, and `update` round-trip the field; existing rows keep `automation = None`. (3) `check_transition_gates` gains 4 new branches: delivery (for each rule in `dest.automation.delivery_rules`, check `require_committed_changes` via `git status --porcelain` on the session's cwd and `require_clean_worktree` via `git diff --quiet`); contract (when `require_canonical_story`, parse a `\`\`\`yaml` code block from `task.description` and assert the required fields are present); checklist (when `required_checklist` is set, scan `task.evidence` for matched `[x]` items — the existing `task.verification_report` text is the natural evidence surface); validator (when `validator_command` is set, run it as a subprocess with a 30s timeout and require `status == 0`; the result is cached on a `kanban_validations` table to avoid re-runs). (4) `gate_mode: blocking` (default) rejects the move on gate failure with a structured `AppError::validation_with_code`; `gate_mode: warning` allows the move but logs a `tracing::warn!` with the gate details. (5) `crates/weave-server/src/api/columns.rs` POST/PATCH handlers accept the `automation` JSON; the move_card tool endpoint returns `{ error: { code, message, gate_type, missing } }` when a gate fails (caller can branch on `gate_type`). (6) Reuse the existing `AppError::validation_with_code` pattern at `service/kanban.rs:192` (the `cwd_outside_codebase` case is the template) for the structured error response. **Budget note:** the validator_command gate needs a subprocess runner (~150 lines for runner + tests) and may push this task over the 1000-line PR budget. If so, defer `validator_command` to a follow-up feature (or split feat-066 into 066a: schema + artifact/delivery/contract gates, and 066b: checklist + validator + gate_mode). Acceptance: `cargo test -p weave-server -- test_check_transition_gates_artifact_gate` (extends the existing 3-gate tests), `test_check_transition_gates_delivery_gate` (uses a `tempfile`-based fake git repo with committed + clean worktree states), `test_check_transition_gates_contract_gate` (parses a sample `\`\`\`yaml` block), `test_check_transition_gates_gate_mode_warning` (warning mode lets the move through and emits a `tracing::warn!`), `test_move_card_gate_failed_error_shape` (asserts the error JSON contains `gate_type` + `missing`). Existing `test_move_card_transition_gates` and `test_check_transition_gates_*` tests stay green (the new branches are additive, gated on `dest.automation.is_some()`).
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_check_transition_gates && cargo test -p weave-server -- test_move_card_gate_failed_error_shape && ./init.sh

features.behavior: Lifecycle supervision for kanban-auto-spawned Claude Code sessions: a tokio watchdog that detects stalled sessions, re-prompts them with a 'you appear stalled, continue' prefix, stops recovery after `max_recovery_retries` (default 2), and broadcasts a `SessionFailed` SSE event on the board channel when the limit is hit. Implementation: (1) New migration `crates/weave-server/src/migrations/016_kanban_session_watch.sql`: a `kanban_session_watch(session_id PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, task_id TEXT NOT NULL, last_activity_at TEXT NOT NULL, recovery_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'watching')` table. The `last_activity_at` is bumped on every SSE event for the session (the `SseManager::broadcast` path is the natural hook) and on every `send_prompt` call. (2) New file `crates/weave-server/src/service/kanban_lifecycle.rs` defines `pub struct KanbanLifecycleSupervisor` with `start(state: AppState, shutdown: CancellationToken) -> JoinHandle<()>` (spawns a `tokio::time::interval` task at 30s, mirrors the orphan-reaping pattern at `service/startup.rs:1`) and `scan_once(&state, now: Instant) -> ScanReport` (pure function, returns the list of stalled sessions, for testability). A session is 'stalled' when `now - last_activity_at > stall_threshold_seconds` (default 300s, per-column override via `column.automation.stall_threshold_seconds` once feat-066 lands). (3) `service/kanban.rs::try_automate_lane` writes the initial `kanban_session_watch` row after creating the session (lines 207-229). (4) On stall detection, the supervisor calls `SessionService::send_prompt(session_id, &format!("You appear stalled (no activity in {seconds}s). Continue the kanban task. If you're done, call `move_card` to advance the card; if you're blocked, call `update_card` with a comment explaining the blocker.\n\n{last_assistant_message_excerpt}"))` and increments `recovery_count`. (5) When `recovery_count > max_recovery_retries`, the supervisor sets the row's `status = 'failed'`, broadcasts `SseWireEvent::SessionFailed { session_id, task_id, reason: 'max_recovery_retries_exceeded' }` on the `board:{board_id}` channel (same pattern as `SessionStarted` at `kanban.rs:249-257`), and stops watching. (6) `crates/weave-server/src/sse/mod.rs` gains the `SessionFailed` variant. (7) `lib.rs::run_cleanup` (or a new helper) stops the supervisor on shutdown within the `CLEANUP_BUDGET` (5s, per `lib.rs:19`). Acceptance: `cargo test -p weave-server -- test_kanban_lifecycle_stall_detected` (fake clock advances past the threshold; supervisor reports 1 stalled session), `test_kanban_lifecycle_recovery_resends_prompt` (asserts `send_prompt` is called with the stalled prefix), `test_kanban_lifecycle_max_retries_emits_failed_event` (asserts `SseWireEvent::SessionFailed` after 2 recovery attempts), `test_kanban_lifecycle_shuts_down_cleanly` (asserts supervisor stops within `CLEANUP_BUDGET`). The conformance test in `tests/cli_conformance.rs` gains a case: a fake CLI emits no events for 6 minutes, the watchdog fires, and the recovery prefix reaches the CLI's stdin. `./init.sh` 3-layer gate green. Line budget: ~800 lines (50 migration + 300 supervisor impl + 50 watch table hookup + 200 lifecycle tests + 100 SSE variant + 100 main.rs spawn).
features.verification: cargo test -p weave-server -- test_kanban_lifecycle && cargo test -p weave-server --test cli_conformance && ./init.sh
